notes: Could not reproduce the reported TypeInitializationException or DllNotFoundException on .NET 10 (SDK 10.0.102) with SkiaSharp 3.119.1 or 3.119.2 when the wasm-tools workload is properly installed. The SkiaSharp.NativeAssets.WebAssembly targets file auto-sets WasmBuildNative=true, which triggers native compilation and links libSkiaSharp.a into dotnet.native.wasm correctly. The error DOES occur when WasmBuildNative is explicitly set to false (confirmed in testing), which prevents native linking. The root cause for reporters is likely: (1) missing wasm-tools workload for .NET 10 (must run 'dotnet workload restore' in the project directory to get the net10.0-compatible workload), or (2) explicit WasmBuildNative=false in their project file. Multiple independent users confirmed these workarounds resolve the issue. Note: .NET 10 wasm-tools uses emscripten 3.1.56, matching SkiaSharp's pre-compiled static libraries.

reproductionSteps.output: Successfully installed workload(s) wasm-tools. Installed emscripten 3.1.56 for SDK 10.0.102.

reproductionSteps.description: Create Blazor WASM project targeting net10.0 with SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor 3.119.1
reproductionSteps.command: dotnet new blazorwasm -n Repro --framework net10.0 && cd Repro && dotnet add package SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor --version 3.119.1
reproductionSteps.output: Restore succeeded. PackageReference for 'SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor' version '3.119.1' added.

filesCreated.description: Razor page with SKCanvasView using reporter's exact drawing code

reproductionSteps.description: Build Blazor WASM project (native compilation links libSkiaSharp.a via emscripten 3.1.56)
reproductionSteps.output: Compiling native assets with emscripten/emcc with -O0. Linking with emcc with -O0. libSkiaSharp.a linked from NativeAssets.WebAssembly 3.119.1 (3.1.56/st,simd). Build succeeded. 0 Warning(s) 0 Error(s). Time Elapsed 00:00:16.25

reproductionSteps.description: Run Blazor WASM app and check browser console for errors — no errors found, rendering succeeded
reproductionSteps.output: Browser console: 'SUCCESS: rendered 800x600'. No errors. SKCanvasView rendered correctly. Page loaded with navigation bar and article content visible.

versionResults.notes: Works correctly on net10.0 with SDK 10.0.102 and wasm-tools installed. SKCanvasView renders, browser console shows SUCCESS. Error reproduces only when WasmBuildNative=false is set.
Latest stable also works correctly on net10.0 with wasm-tools installed. Same behavior as 3.119.1.

actionability.reason: Could not reproduce with reporter's exact project configuration on .NET 10 SDK 10.0.102 with wasm-tools workload installed. The error only occurs when WasmBuildNative=false or when wasm-tools is missing. Reporter may have had missing workload or explicit WasmBuildNative=false. Multiple users confirmed these workarounds resolve the issue.

actions.description: Ask reporter to confirm their wasm-tools workload status and WasmBuildNative setting

workarounds: Run 'dotnet workload restore' in the project directory to install wasm-tools for .NET 10 (installs emscripten 3.1.56)
Ensure WasmBuildNative is NOT explicitly set to false in the .csproj — SkiaSharp's targets auto-set it to true
If WasmBuildNative was false, remove it and add <RunAOTCompilation>false</RunAOTCompilation> instead
missingInfo: Output of 'dotnet workload list' on the reporter's machine
Full build output showing whether 'Compiling native assets with emscripten' step ran

proposedResponse.body:
Thanks for the report. We attempted to reproduce this on Linux x64 with .NET SDK 10.0.102 and SkiaSharp 3.119.1 / 3.119.2 targeting `net10.0`, but the app works correctly when the `wasm-tools` workload is properly installed.

**What we found:**
- The `TypeInitialization_Type, SkiaSharp.SKImageInfo` error occurs when **native WASM compilation is skipped** — meaning `libSkiaSharp.a` was never linked into `dotnet.native.wasm`.
- This happens when either: (1) the `wasm-tools` workload is not installed for .NET 10, or (2) `<WasmBuildNative>false</WasmBuildNative>` is explicitly set in the project.
- SkiaSharp's targets file auto-sets `WasmBuildNative=true`, so it should work automatically — but only if the wasm-tools workload is installed.

**Workarounds:**
1. Run `dotnet workload restore` in your project directory (this installs the wasm-tools workload for .NET 10 with emscripten 3.1.56)
2. Ensure your `.csproj` does NOT have `<WasmBuildNative>false</WasmBuildNative>`
3. If you previously had `WasmBuildNative=false`, remove it and add `<RunAOTCompilation>false</RunAOTCompilation>` instead

**Could you share:**
- Output of `dotnet workload list`
- Whether your build output includes the line "Compiling native assets with emscripten"
- Whether any `.csproj` property explicitly sets `WasmBuildNative`

**Tested versions:**
| Version | .NET 10 Result |
|---------|---------------|
| 3.119.1 | ✅ Works (with wasm-tools) |
| 3.119.2 | ✅ Works (with wasm-tools) |

corrections.upstream: SkiaSharp's pre-compiled emscripten static libraries (built for 3.1.56) may be incompatible with .NET 10's updated emscripten toolchain
corrections.corrected: .NET 10's wasm-tools workload also uses emscripten 3.1.56, same as SkiaSharp's static libs. The issue is not emscripten version mismatch but missing wasm-tools workload or WasmBuildNative=false.
